{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2002 Cannes Film Festival | image = CFF2002poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 55th Cannes Film Festival.{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2002/posters.html |title=Posters 2002 |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131111025128/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2002/posters.html |archive-date=11 November 2013 |df=dmy}} | host = [[Virginie Ledoyen]] | number = 22 (Main Competition) | opening = ''[[Hollywood Ending]]'' | closing = ''[[And Now... Ladies and Gentlemen|And Now... Ladies
and Gentlemen]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[The Pianist (2002 film)|The Pianist]]'' | date = {{Start date|2002|05|15|df=y}} – {{End date|2002|05|26|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2001 Cannes Film Festival|2001]] | next = [[2003 Cannes Film Festival|2003]] }} The '''55th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 15 to 26 May 2002.{{cite web |title=55ème Festival International du Film - Cannes |url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2002.htm |access-date=20 June 2017 |work=cinema-francais.fr |language=fr}}{{cite web |title=Cannes 2002 Chroniques |url=http://www.cannes-fest.com/2002/palm.htm |access-date=20 June 2017 |work=cannes-fest.com |language=fr}}{{cite web |title=2002 Cannes Film Festival |url=http://www.infoplease.com/ipa/A0906761.html |access-date=25 May 2017}} American filmmaker [[David Lynch]] served as jury president for the main competition. [[Virginie Ledoyen]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies.{{cite news |title=Ledoyen to reign again |url=https://variety.com/2002/film/news/ledoyen-to-reign-again-1117865015/ |access-date=25 May 2017 |work=Variety.com}} French-Polish filmmaker [[Roman Polanski]] won the ''[[Palme d'Or]]'', the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[The Pianist (2002 film)|The Pianist]]''. American filmmaker [[Woody Allen]] was awarded with the inaugural [[Honorary Palme d'Or]], given to a director who had achieved a notable body of work but who had never won the regular [[Palme d'Or]].{{cite web |title=A Honorary Palme at the opening ceremony of the Festival de Cannes |url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/58029.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160124000809/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/article/58029.html |archive-date=24 January 2016 |work=festival-cannes.fr |df=dmy}} The festival opened with ''[[Hollywood Ending]]'' by [[Woody Allen]],{{cite web |url=https://www.screendaily.com/hollywood-ending-gets-its-international-fest-premiere-before-cannes-at-san-francisco/408937.article |title=Hollywood Ending gets its international fest premiere before Cannes at San Francisco |work=screendaily.com |access-date=25 May 2017 |quote=needs subscription}} and closed with [[Claude Lelouch]]'s ''[[And Now... Ladies and Gentlemen]]''.{{cite web |url=https://www.indiewire.com/article/festivals_2002_cannes_film_festival_special_screenings_lineup |title=Festivals: 2002 Cannes Film Festival Special Screenings Lineup |work=indiewire.com |date=24 March 2001 |access-date=25 May 2017}}{{cite news|url=https://variety.com/2002/film/news/lelouch-ladies-to-end-cannes-1117865628/ |title=Lelouch 'Ladies' to end Cannes |work=Variety.com |access-date=25 May 2017}}{{cite web |url=http://www.urbancinefile.com.au/home/view.asp?a=6154&s=features |title=Cannes 2002 – A Preview |work=urbancinefile.com |access-date=25 May 2017 |archive-date=14 December 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214080446/http://www.urbancinefile.com.au/home/view.asp?a=6154&s=features |url-status=dead The organizers of the 2002 festival assembled a jury of six members, including [[Dieter Kosslick]] and [[Alberto Barbera]], to watch seven of the twelve features which had been entered in the 1939 competition, namely: ''[[Goodbye, Mr. Chips (1939 film)|Goodbye, Mr. Chips]]'', ''[[La Loi du Nord|La piste du nord]]'', ''[[Lenin in 1918]]'', ''[[The Four Feathers (1939 film)|The Four Feathers]]'', ''[[The Wizard of Oz (1939 film)|The Wizard of Oz]]'', ''[[Union Pacific (film)|Union Pacific]]'', and ''[[Boefje]]''. ''Union Pacific'' was retrospectively voted the winner of the 1939 Palme d'Or.{{cite web |last1=Vaucher |first1=Andrea R. |last2=Elley |first2=Derek |date=24 April 2002 |title=Croisette crowd craves its faves |url=https://variety.com/2002/film/markets-festivals/croisette-crowd-craves-its-faves-1117865929/ |access-date=18 November 2019 |website=Variety |language=en}}; {{Cite web |last=McCarthy |first=Todd |date=May 26, 2002 |title='Pianist' tickles Cannes |url=https://variety.com/2002/film/markets-festivals/pianist-tickles-cannes-1117867622/ |access-date=May 24, 2018 |work=[[Variety
